post.description: Start a new authentication intent on configured identity provider (IDP) for external login, registration or linking.

get.summary: Get the information of the IDP authentication intent
get.description: Get the information returned by the identity provider (IDP) for registration or updating an existing user with new information.

parameters.description: ID of the identity provider (IDP) intent, previously returned on the success response of the start identity provider intent.

post.description: Create a new user with an optional data schema.

Search all matching users. By default, we will return all users of your instance.
 Make sure to include a limit and sorting for pagination.

parameters.name: query.desc
parameters.description: If desc is true, the result is sorted by in descending order. Beware that if desc is true or the sorting column is not the creation date, the pagination results might be inconsistent.

parameters.name: sortingColumn
parameters.description: the field the result is sorted.

requestBody.description: Define the criteria to query for.

schema.title: filters
schema.description: Define the criteria to query for.

get.description: Returns the user identified by the requested ID.

delete.description:
Delete an existing user and change the state to 'deleted'.
 The user will be able to log in anymore.

patch.description: Patch an existing user with data based on a user schema.

post.description:
Activate a previously deactivated user and change the state to 'active'.
 The user will be able to log in again.

 The endpoint returns an error if the user is not in the state 'deactivated'.

Deactivate an existing user and change the state 'deactivated'.
 The user will not be able to log in anymore.
 Use deactivate user when the user should not be able to use the account anymore,
 but you still need access to the user data.

 The endpoint returns an error if the user is already in the state 'deactivated'.

Lock an existing user and change the state 'locked'.
 The user will not be able to log in anymore.
 Use lock user when the user should temporarily not be able to log in
 because of an event that happened (wrong password, etc.)

 The endpoint returns an error if the user is already in the state 'locked'.

Unlock a previously locked user and change the state to 'active'.
 The user will be able to log in again.

 The endpoint returns an error if the user is not in the state 'locked'.

put.description:
Add or update the contact email address of a user.
 If the email is not passed as verified, a verification code will be generated,
 which can be either returned or will be sent to the user by email.

requestBody.description: Set the user's contact email and it's verification state.

schema.description: Set the user's contact email and it's verification state.

post.description: Resend the email with the verification code for the contact email address.

post.description:
Start the registration of a new WebAuthN device (e.g. Passkeys) for a user.
 As a response the public key credential creation options are returned,
 which are used to verify the device.

Create a link, which includes a code, that can either be returned or directly sent to the user.
 The code will allow the user to start a new WebAuthN registration.

values.title: values
values.description: Repeated field of dynamically typed values.

google.protobuf.ListValue.title: ListValue
google.protobuf.ListValue.description:
`ListValue` is a wrapper around a repeated field of values.

 The JSON representation for `ListValue` is JSON array.

google.protobuf.NullValue.title: NullValue
google.protobuf.NullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
 `Value` type union.

 The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

google.protobuf.Struct.description:
`Struct` represents a structured data value, consisting of fields
 which map to dynamically typed values. In some languages, `Struct`
 might be supported by a native representation. For example, in
 scripting languages like JS a struct is represented as an
 object. The details of that representation are described together
 with the proto support for the language.

 The JSON representation for `Struct` is JSON object.

google.protobuf.Value.description:
`Value` represents a dynamically typed value which can be either
 null, a number, a string, a boolean, a recursive struct value, or a
 list of values. A producer of value is expected to set one of these
 variants. Absence of any variant indicates an error.

 The JSON representation for `Value` is JSON value.

andFilter.title: and_filter
andFilter.description:
Limit the result to match all sub queries ('AND').
 Note that if you specify multiple queries, they will be implicitly used as andQueries.
 Use the andFilter in combination with orFilter and notFilter.

emailFilter.title: email_filter
emailFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific contact email.

notFilter.title: not_filter
notFilter.description: Exclude / Negate the result of the sub query ('NOT').

orFilter.title: or_filter
orFilter.description: Union the results of each sub filter ('OR').

organizationIdFilter.title: organization_id_filter
organizationIdFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific organization.

phoneFilter.title: phone_filter
phoneFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific contact phone.

schemaIdFilter.title: schema_id_filter
schemaIdFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific schema ID.

schemaTypeFilter.title: schema_type_filter
schemaTypeFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific schema type.

stateFilter.title: state_filter
stateFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific state of the user.

userIdFilter.title: user_id_filter
userIdFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific user ID.

usernameFilter.title: username_filter
usernameFilter.description: Limit the result to a specific username.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description: the field the result is sorted.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Define the criteria to query for.

details.title: details
details.description: Details provides information about the returned result including total amount found.

result.title: result
result.description: The result contains the user schemas, which matched the queries.

username.description: Set the user's username. This will be used for identification during authentication.

isOrganizationSpecific.title: is_organization_specific
isOrganizationSpecific.description:
By default username must be unique across all organizations in an instance.
 This option allow to restrict the uniqueness to the user's own organization.
 As a result, this username can only be used if the authentication is limited
 to the corresponding organization.

 This can be useful if you provide multiple usernames for a single user, where one
 if specific to your organization, e.g.:
 - gigi-giraffe@zitadel.com (unique across organizations)
 - gigi-giraffe (unique only inside the Zitadel organization)

idpIntent.title: idp_intent
idpIntent.description:
The Start Intent directly succeeded and returned the IDP Intent.
 Further information can be retrieved by using the retrieve identity provider intent request.

postForm.title: post_form
postForm.description: The HTML form with the embedded POST call information to render and execute.

organization.title: organization
organization.description: Optionally expect the user to be in this organization.

totpId.title: totp_id
totpId.description: unique identifier of the TOTP registration.

uri.title: uri
uri.description: The TOTP URI, which can be used to create a QR Code for scanning with an authenticator app.

secret.title: secret
secret.description: The TOTP secret, which can be used for manually adding in an authenticator app.

domain.title: domain
domain.description: Domain on which the user currently is or will be authenticated.

authenticatorType.title: authenticator_type
authenticatorType.description:
Optionally specify the authenticator type of the passkey device (platform or cross-platform).
 If none is provided, both values are allowed.

code.title: code
code.description:
Optionally provide a one time code generated by ZITADEL.
 This is required to start the passkey registration without user authentication.

organization.title: organization
organization.description: Optionally expect the user to be in this organization.

webAuthNId.title: web_auth_n_id
webAuthNId.description: unique identifier of the WebAuthN registration.

publicKeyCredentialCreationOptions.title: public_key_credential_creation_options
publicKeyCredentialCreationOptions.description:
Options for Credential Creation (dictionary PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions).
 Generated helper methods transform the field to JSON, for use in a WebauthN client.
 See also:  https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/#dictdef-publickeycredentialcreationoptions

state.title: state
state.description: Defines the state to query for.

totpId.title: totp_id
totpId.description: unique identifier of the TOTP authenticator.

name.title: name
name.description: The name provided during registration. This is used for easier identification.

isVerified.title: is_verified
isVerified.description: State whether the TOTP registration has been completed.

organization.title: organization
organization.description: Optionally expect the user to be in this organization.

id.title: id
id.description: Defines the ID of the user to query for.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text comparison method used for the id query.

username.title: username
username.description: The user's unique username. It is used for identification during authentication.

isOrganizationSpecific.title: is_organization_specific
isOrganizationSpecific.description:
By default usernames must be unique across all organizations in an instance.
 This option allow to restrict the uniqueness to the user's own organization.
 As a result, this username can only be used if the authentication is limited
 to the corresponding organization.

 This can be useful if you provide multiple usernames for a single user, where one
 if specific to your organization, e.g.:
 - gigi-giraffe@zitadel.com (unique across organizations)
 - gigi-giraffe (unique only inside the Zitadel organization)

username.title: username
username.description: Defines the username to query for.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text comparison method used for the username query.

isOrganizationSpecific.title: is_organization_specific
isOrganizationSpecific.description: Defines that the username must only be unique in the organisation.

verificationCode.description: Set the verification code generated during the set contact email request.

publicKeyCredential.title: public_key_credential
publicKeyCredential.description:
PublicKeyCredential Interface.
 Generated helper methods populate the field from JSON created by a WebAuthN client.
 See also:  https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/#publickeycredential

webAuthNName.title: web_auth_n_name
webAuthNName.description: Provide a name for the WebAuthN device. This will help identify it in the future.

organization.title: organization
organization.description: Optionally expect the user to be in this organization.

webAuthNId.title: web_auth_n_id
webAuthNId.description: unique identifier of the WebAuthN registration, which was returned in the start webauthn registration.

webAuthNId.title: web_auth_n_id
webAuthNId.description: unique identifier of the WebAuthN authenticator.

name.title: name
name.description: Name of the WebAuthN authenticator. This is used for easier identification.

isVerified.title: is_verified
isVerified.description: State whether the WebAuthN registration has been completed.

userVerified.title: user_verified
userVerified.description:
States if the user has been verified during the registration. Authentication with this device
 will be considered as multi-factor authentication (MFA) without the need to check a password
 (typically known as Passkeys).
 Without user verification it will be a second factor authentication (2FA), typically done
 after a password check.

 More on WebAuthN User Verification: https://www.w3.org/TR/webauthn/#user-verification
